PAGE 001 OF 500
TITLE: Prime Gap Resonance Project: 500-Page Status-Preserving Paper
PART: Front Matter and Global Status
STATUS: STRUCTURAL / OVERVIEW
OBJECT: Project identity

This pure-text paper is a status-preserving expansion of the Prime Gap Resonance Project ledger. It is not a proof of the original positive existence problem and it is not a proof of the all-alpha no-positive-limit theorem. It collects the structural obstruction architecture, the conditional finite-type theorem package, the s=2 rational-major endpoint compression, the direct residual-cube solve target, a GitHub documentation plan, and a Codex continuation prompt.

Repository documentation target: https://github.com/flaritycat/PrimeResonanceGap

Original positive existence problem: OPEN.
All-alpha no-positive-limit theorem: OPEN.
Metric theorem A_alpha(x)->1 for Lebesgue-a.e. irrational alpha: PROVEN according to the project ledger.
Finite-type no-positive-limit theorem: CONDITIONAL.
The s=2 rational-major analytic endpoint RBDH_pair_short / CPC_3^sharp / AU^3: OPEN.

The governing rule is simple enough that it will still be violated by someone: never convert a
reduction into a theorem, never convert a conditional implication into an unconditional theorem,
and never convert a model selector into the actual sharp moving selector without the full
transfer package.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.


PAGE 002 OF 500
TITLE: Global status dashboard
PART: Front Matter and Global Status
STATUS: OPEN / PROVEN / CONDITIONAL MIX
OBJECT: A_alpha, finite-type alpha, metric alpha

Original positive existence problem: OPEN.
All-alpha no-positive-limit theorem: OPEN.
Metric theorem A_alpha(x)->1 for Lebesgue-a.e. irrational alpha: PROVEN according to the project ledger.
Finite-type no-positive-limit theorem: CONDITIONAL.
The s=2 rational-major analytic endpoint RBDH_pair_short / CPC_3^sharp / AU^3: OPEN.

The strongest unconditional global statement recorded by the ledger is the metric theorem: A_alpha(x)->1 for Lebesgue-a.e. irrational alpha. This does not classify exceptional alpha. It does not rule out a positive construction. It does not prove a universal negative theorem. The finite-type route is conditional on matched endpoint engines, and the s=2 rational-major branch is compressed to one open analytic endpoint class.

The paper therefore uses the word CONDITIONAL with intent, not modesty. Removing that word
without proving the engines is theorem laundering, now with more pages.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.


PAGE 003 OF 500
TITLE: Claim discipline
PART: Front Matter and Global Status
STATUS: REQUIRED DISCIPLINE
OBJECT: Status labels and forbidden upgrades

Use only these status labels unless stronger proof is supplied: PROVEN, CONDITIONAL, STRUCTURAL
/ EXTRACTION, HEURISTIC, OPEN, FALSE / BLOCKED. Never upgrade a smoothed, frozen, finite-stage,
or model statement into a theorem for the actual sharp moving selector unless boundary,
transition, denominator, prefix, tail, and moving-window transfer are explicitly supplied.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.


PAGE 004 OF 500
TITLE: Core objects
PART: Front Matter and Global Status
STATUS: DEFINITION
OBJECT: Prime gaps, selector, denominator, numerator

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.


PAGE 005 OF 500
TITLE: Actual selector discipline
PART: Front Matter and Global Status
STATUS: REQUIRED DISCIPLINE
OBJECT: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}

The actual selector is sharp and moving. It depends on p through the boundary 1/log p. Frozen
dyadic, smoothed finite-band, hidden Bernoulli, finite-stage, survivor, core-floor, and
rebalanced measures are useful models, not the original problem. Any theorem about one of them
must keep its name tag on. The transfer package includes boundary transfer, moving-window
control, transition control, denominator divergence, prefix safety, tail control, and W-trick
compatibility.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.


PAGE 006 OF 500
TITLE: Gap-object discipline
PART: Front Matter and Global Status
STATUS: REQUIRED DISCIPLINE
OBJECT: full, clipped, tail, threshold, tuple

Every theorem must say whether it uses the full gap g(p), clipped gap g_H(p)=min(g(p),H), centered clipped gap, tail tau_H(p)=(g(p)-H)_+, threshold indicator 1_{g(p)>y}, empty-interval indicator 1_{Y_p(h)=0}, or tuple-count array binom(Y_p(h),j). Fixed-order tuple neutrality is not high-count control. Global mean-one normalized gaps are not local tail uniform integrability. Humanity may dislike this many cases, but gaps do not care.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.


PAGE 007 OF 500
TITLE: What this paper is not
PART: Front Matter and Global Status
STATUS: REQUIRED DISCIPLINE
OBJECT: No solved original problem

This paper does not claim that there exists an irrational alpha with A_alpha(x)->L0>1. It does not claim that no irrational alpha can have A_alpha(x)->L0>1. It does not claim an unconditional finite-type theorem. It does not prove RBDH_pair_short, CPC_3^sharp, or AU^3. It is a paper-length ledger, theorem architecture, endpoint map, and continuation protocol.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

PAGE 010 OF 500
TITLE: Dependency firewall
PART: Front Matter and Global Status
STATUS: STRUCTURAL / OVERVIEW
OBJECT: Dependency firewall

This page belongs to Front Matter and Global Status. Its topic is Dependency firewall. The
purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant definitions,
assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a status-
preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. The named object can be used only with its stated selector, gap type, local model,
normalization, and range. If it is structural, it extracts data or an obstruction; if it is
conditional, it requires named engines; if it is open, it identifies a target rather than a
theorem.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.


PAGE 011 OF 500
TITLE: Current best analytic target
PART: Front Matter and Global Status
STATUS: OPEN ANALYTIC ENGINE
OBJECT: RBDH_pair_short / CPC_3^sharp / AU^3

For W-tricked nu and P_d(n)=nu(n+d)conj(nu(n)), the endpoint RBDH_pair_short(Hcal) asks, uniformly for dyadic D<=Hcal(N),

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} (1/N) sum_h |
  N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N) P_d(n+h)conj(P_d(n))
  - Sigma_w(d,h) N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N)
|^2 = o(1).

The exact rectangle model is
Sigma_w(d,h)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-4)(1-#{0,-d,-h,-h-d mod p}/p).

The centered pair-cube formulation uses R_d(n)=P_d(n)-kappa_w(d), where
kappa_w(d)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-2)(1-#{0,-d mod p}/p).
The endpoint CPC_3^sharp(Hcal) asks for
(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||R_d||_{U^2([N])}^4=o(1),
with sharp or transferred dyadic cutoffs and all side packages.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: Paper roadmap
PART: Front Matter and Global Status
STATUS: STRUCTURAL / OVERVIEW
OBJECT: Three-paper split and continuation architecture

The paper is organized into seven parts. Part I is the structural obstruction theorem. Part II is the conditional finite-type theorem. Part III is the s=2 rational-major endpoint compression. Part IV isolates the direct residual-cube analytic target. Part V gives computational experiments, which are diagnostics and not proofs. Part VI gives the GitHub documentation plan. Part VII gives the Codex continuation prompt. Appendices collect status labels, endpoint tables, source engines, and forbidden shortcuts.

prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

OBJECT: Clipped covariance branch

This page belongs to Part I: Structural Obstruction Paper. Its topic is Clipped covariance
branch. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-

estimates for free.

This page belongs to Part I: Structural Obstruction Paper. Its topic is Actual sharp moving
selector. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Selector warning: the actual selector is sharp and moving; frozen and smoothed versions require transfer.

STATUS: STRUCTURAL / EXTRACTION
OBJECT: Empty-interval conversion

This page belongs to Part I: Structural Obstruction Paper. Its topic is Empty-interval
conversion. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Tail discipline: tau_H(p)=(g(p)-H)_+. Layer-cake gives tau_H(p)=int_H^infty 1_{g(p)>y} dy. The event g(p)>y is the empty interval condition that (p,p+y log p] contains no prime. Bonferroni expansion produces fixed tuple arrays plus a high-count remainder supported on Y_p(h)>=J+1.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or

prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,

TITLE: Paper I final theorem
PART: Part I: Structural Obstruction Paper
STATUS: PROVEN STRUCTURAL / EXTRACTION
OBJECT: Positive finite selected limit implies obstruction branches

Assume D_alpha(x)->infinity and A_alpha(x)->1+delta with delta>0. Then for every fixed clipping threshold H, infinitely many dyadic shells carry persistent q-centered clipped covariance or q-centered tail covariance. The theorem extracts obstruction. It does not eliminate obstruction. The branch map then routes tail covariance through threshold covariance, empty intervals, tuple arrays, high-count remainders, prefix-dangerous exclusions, moving windows, and large-range alternatives.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: Finite-type Diophantine setup
PART: Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper
STATUS: CONDITIONAL / STRUCTURAL
OBJECT: Finite-type Diophantine setup

This page belongs to Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper. Its topic is Finite-type
Diophantine setup. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Finite-type screen: if ||u alpha + a/q|| <= rho with q<=Q and |u|<=U, then finite type ||r alpha||>=c_alpha r^{-tau_alpha} forces Q^{tau_alpha+1} U^{tau_alpha} rho >= c_alpha. If this inequality fails, the certificate cannot exist. This screen does not apply to Liouville alpha.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-

estimates for free.

Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

OBJECT: Tail-threshold source envelope

This page belongs to Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper. Its topic is Tail-threshold source

Tail discipline: tau_H(p)=(g(p)-H)_+. Layer-cake gives tau_H(p)=int_H^infty 1_{g(p)>y} dy. The event g(p)>y is the empty interval condition that (p,p+y log p] contains no prime. Bonferroni expansion produces fixed tuple arrays plus a high-count remainder supported on Y_p(h)>=J+1.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

OBJECT: Rational-major source envelope

This page belongs to Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper. Its topic is Rational-major source

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or

prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Finite-type screen: if ||u alpha + a/q|| <= rho with q<=Q and |u|<=U, then finite type ||r alpha||>=c_alpha r^{-tau_alpha} forces Q^{tau_alpha+1} U^{tau_alpha} rho >= c_alpha. If this inequality fails, the certificate cannot exist. This screen does not apply to Liouville alpha.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or

TITLE: Tail-threshold source envelope

STATUS: CONDITIONAL / STRUCTURAL
OBJECT: Tail-threshold source envelope

This page belongs to Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper. Its topic is Tail-threshold source
envelope. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Tail discipline: tau_H(p)=(g(p)-H)_+. Layer-cake gives tau_H(p)=int_H^infty 1_{g(p)>y} dy. The event g(p)>y is the empty interval condition that (p,p+y log p] contains no prime. Bonferroni expansion produces fixed tuple arrays plus a high-count remainder supported on Y_p(h)>=J+1.

TITLE: Finite-type Diophantine setup
PART: Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper
STATUS: CONDITIONAL / STRUCTURAL
OBJECT: Finite-type Diophantine setup

This page belongs to Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper. Its topic is Finite-type
Diophantine setup. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Finite-type screen: if ||u alpha + a/q|| <= rho with q<=Q and |u|<=U, then finite type ||r alpha||>=c_alpha r^{-tau_alpha} forces Q^{tau_alpha+1} U^{tau_alpha} rho >= c_alpha. If this inequality fails, the certificate cannot exist. This screen does not apply to Liouville alpha.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: Source taxonomy
PART: Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper
STATUS: CONDITIONAL / STRUCTURAL
OBJECT: Source taxonomy

This page belongs to Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper. Its topic is Source taxonomy. The
purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant definitions,
assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a status-
preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

TITLE: Tail-threshold source envelope

STATUS: CONDITIONAL / STRUCTURAL
OBJECT: Tail-threshold source envelope

This page belongs to Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper. Its topic is Tail-threshold source
envelope. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or

Tail discipline: tau_H(p)=(g(p)-H)_+. Layer-cake gives tau_H(p)=int_H^infty 1_{g(p)>y} dy. The event g(p)>y is the empty interval condition that (p,p+y log p] contains no prime. Bonferroni expansion produces fixed tuple arrays plus a high-count remainder supported on Y_p(h)>=J+1.

STATUS: CONDITIONAL / STRUCTURAL
OBJECT: W-trick source envelope

This page belongs to Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper. Its topic is W-trick source
envelope. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

W-trick discipline: p=Wn+b, W=prod_{p<=w}p, and W=N^{o(1)} under the limit order N->infty first, then w->infty. Original offsets convert to W-shifts only when residue-compatible, and Fourier carriers acquire W factors.

required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

OBJECT: Consolidated range theorem

This page belongs to Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper. Its topic is Consolidated range
theorem. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

TITLE: Tail-threshold source envelope
PART: Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper
STATUS: CONDITIONAL / STRUCTURAL
OBJECT: Tail-threshold source envelope

This page belongs to Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper. Its topic is Tail-threshold source
envelope. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Tail discipline: tau_H(p)=(g(p)-H)_+. Layer-cake gives tau_H(p)=int_H^infty 1_{g(p)>y} dy. The event g(p)>y is the empty interval condition that (p,p+y log p] contains no prime. Bonferroni expansion produces fixed tuple arrays plus a high-count remainder supported on Y_p(h)>=J+1.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-

subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

OBJECT: Conditional finite-type theorem

This page belongs to Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper. Its topic is Conditional finite-

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or

prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Finite-type screen: if ||u alpha + a/q|| <= rho with q<=Q and |u|<=U, then finite type ||r alpha||>=c_alpha r^{-tau_alpha} forces Q^{tau_alpha+1} U^{tau_alpha} rho >= c_alpha. If this inequality fails, the certificate cannot exist. This screen does not apply to Liouville alpha.

Finite-type screen: if ||u alpha + a/q|| <= rho with q<=Q and |u|<=U, then finite type ||r alpha||>=c_alpha r^{-tau_alpha} forces Q^{tau_alpha+1} U^{tau_alpha} rho >= c_alpha. If this inequality fails, the certificate cannot exist. This screen does not apply to Liouville alpha.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

TITLE: Tail-threshold source envelope

STATUS: CONDITIONAL / STRUCTURAL
OBJECT: Tail-threshold source envelope

This page belongs to Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper. Its topic is Tail-threshold source
envelope. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or

prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Tail discipline: tau_H(p)=(g(p)-H)_+. Layer-cake gives tau_H(p)=int_H^infty 1_{g(p)>y} dy. The event g(p)>y is the empty interval condition that (p,p+y log p] contains no prime. Bonferroni expansion produces fixed tuple arrays plus a high-count remainder supported on Y_p(h)>=J+1.

PAGE 145 OF 500
TITLE: Paper II final theorem
PART: Part II: Conditional Finite-Type Paper
STATUS: CONDITIONAL
OBJECT: Source-matched finite-type no-positive-limit theorem

Let alpha be irrational of finite Diophantine type. Suppose Paper I structural extraction holds,
every active packet is source-labeled, every source envelope D_sigma(N) is certified, every
matched source engine is proved in the required range, and all transfer packages hold. Then
A_alpha(x) cannot converge to a finite L0>1. This is the conditional finite-type theorem. It
remains conditional because RBDH/CPC/AU^3, DPLS, HLSV, TailClose, HCLOSE, BdryClose, WClose, and
large-range controls are not all proved.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||R_d||_{U^2([N])}^4=o(1),

TITLE: Rectangle model Sigma_w
PART: Part III: s=2 Rational-Major Endpoint Compression
STATUS: STRUCTURAL / CONDITIONAL EQUIVALENCE
OBJECT: Rectangle model Sigma_w

This page belongs to Part III: s=2 Rational-Major Endpoint Compression. Its topic is Rectangle model Sigma_w. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

For W-tricked nu and P_d(n)=nu(n+d)conj(nu(n)), the endpoint RBDH_pair_short(Hcal) asks, uniformly for dyadic D<=Hcal(N),

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} (1/N) sum_h |
  N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N) P_d(n+h)conj(P_d(n))
  - Sigma_w(d,h) N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N)
|^2 = o(1).

The exact rectangle model is
Sigma_w(d,h)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-4)(1-#{0,-d,-h,-h-d mod p}/p).

 Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source

STATUS: STRUCTURAL / CONDITIONAL EQUIVALENCE
OBJECT: Two-rectangle model Theta_w

This page belongs to Part III: s=2 Rational-Major Endpoint Compression. Its topic is Two-rectangle model Theta_w. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

For W-tricked nu and P_d(n)=nu(n+d)conj(nu(n)), the endpoint RBDH_pair_short(Hcal) asks, uniformly for dyadic D<=Hcal(N),

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} (1/N) sum_h |
  N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N) P_d(n+h)conj(P_d(n))
  - Sigma_w(d,h) N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N)
|^2 = o(1).

The exact rectangle model is
Sigma_w(d,h)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-4)(1-#{0,-d,-h,-h-d mod p}/p).

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or

prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-

subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-

completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

TITLE: Blocked shortcuts
PART: Part III: s=2 Rational-Major Endpoint Compression
STATUS: STRUCTURAL / CONDITIONAL EQUIVALENCE
OBJECT: Blocked shortcuts

This page belongs to Part III: s=2 Rational-Major Endpoint Compression. Its topic is Blocked shortcuts. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

STATUS: STRUCTURAL / CONDITIONAL EQUIVALENCE
OBJECT: WHL4/WHL8 route

This page belongs to Part III: s=2 Rational-Major Endpoint Compression. Its topic is WHL4/WHL8 route. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-

The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or

prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

The centered pair-cube formulation uses R_d(n)=P_d(n)-kappa_w(d), where
kappa_w(d)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-2)(1-#{0,-d mod p}/p).
The endpoint CPC_3^sharp(Hcal) asks for
(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||R_d||_{U^2([N])}^4=o(1),
with sharp or transferred dyadic cutoffs and all side packages.

complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

This page belongs to Part III: s=2 Rational-Major Endpoint Compression. Its topic is Rectangle model Sigma_w. (1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} (1/N) sum_h |
  N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N) P_d(n+h)conj(P_d(n))
  - Sigma_w(d,h) N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N)
|^2 = o(1).

The exact rectangle model is
Sigma_w(d,h)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-4)(1-#{0,-d,-h,-h-d mod p}/p).

estimates for free.

This page belongs to Part III: s=2 Rational-Major Endpoint Compression. Its topic is Equivalence theorem. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or

prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-

q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

For W-tricked nu and P_d(n)=nu(n+d)conj(nu(n)), the endpoint RBDH_pair_short(Hcal) asks, uniformly for dyadic D<=Hcal(N),

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} (1/N) sum_h |
  N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N) P_d(n+h)conj(P_d(n))
  - Sigma_w(d,h) N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N)
|^2 = o(1).

The exact rectangle model is
Sigma_w(d,h)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-4)(1-#{0,-d,-h,-h-d mod p}/p).

 Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

TITLE: Equivalence theorem

STATUS: STRUCTURAL / CONDITIONAL EQUIVALENCE
OBJECT: Equivalence theorem

This page belongs to Part III: s=2 Rational-Major Endpoint Compression. Its topic is Equivalence theorem. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

|^2 = o(1).

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit

range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

|^2 = o(1).

Modulo exact local models, covariance calibration, pair-margin absorption, linear U^2, boundary transfer, collision control, W-trick limit order, prime-power transfer, and range coverage, the s=2 rational-major endpoint formulations RBDH_pair_short, CPC_3^sharp, SPAC_2^sharp, SU2Pair_2^sharp, DyadicDerivativeU^2, and AU^3 are equivalent. TITLE: Expansion of R_d
PART: Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint
STATUS: OPEN ANALYTIC ENGINE / REDUCTION
OBJECT: Expansion of R_d

This page belongs to Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint. Its topic is
Expansion of R_d. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: Linear U^2 terms
PART: Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint
STATUS: OPEN ANALYTIC ENGINE / REDUCTION
OBJECT: Linear U^2 terms

This page belongs to Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint. Its topic is Linear
U^2 terms. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

The centered pair-cube formulation uses R_d(n)=P_d(n)-kappa_w(d), where
kappa_w(d)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-2)(1-#{0,-d mod p}/p).
The endpoint CPC_3^sharp(Hcal) asks for
(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||R_d||_{U^2([N])}^4=o(1),
with sharp or transferred dyadic cutoffs and all side packages.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||B_d^circ||_{U^2}^4=o(1).
Expanding U^2 gives an eight-form residual cube on vertices n+epsilon_1 d+epsilon_2 h+epsilon_3 k, epsilon in {0,1}^3, with zero-frequency subtraction |c_d|^4 and residual local model Omega_w obtained by inclusion-exclusion over all subconfigurations.

the relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read
as part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||B_d^circ||_{U^2}^4=o(1).
Expanding U^2 gives an eight-form residual cube on vertices n+epsilon_1 d+epsilon_2 h+epsilon_3 k, epsilon in {0,1}^3, with zero-frequency subtraction |c_d|^4 and residual local model Omega_w obtained by inclusion-exclusion over all subconfigurations.

TITLE: Linear U^2 terms

STATUS: OPEN ANALYTIC ENGINE / REDUCTION
OBJECT: Linear U^2 terms

This page belongs to Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint. Its topic is Linear
U^2 terms. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

The centered pair-cube formulation uses R_d(n)=P_d(n)-kappa_w(d), where
kappa_w(d)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-2)(1-#{0,-d mod p}/p).
The endpoint CPC_3^sharp(Hcal) asks for
(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||R_d||_{U^2([N])}^4=o(1),
with sharp or transferred dyadic cutoffs and all side packages.

prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without

estimates for free.

TITLE: Residual cube solve target
PART: Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint
STATUS: OPEN ANALYTIC ENGINE
OBJECT: ResCube_3^sharp

The direct derivative target sets f=nu-1, B_d(n)=f(n+d)conj(f(n)), c_d=E_n B_d(n), and B_d^circ=B_d-c_d. The derivative endpoint is
(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||B_d^circ||_{U^2}^4=o(1).
Expanding U^2 gives an eight-form residual cube on vertices n+epsilon_1 d+epsilon_2 h+epsilon_3 k, epsilon in {0,1}^3, with zero-frequency subtraction |c_d|^4 and residual local model Omega_w obtained by inclusion-exclusion over all subconfigurations.

The exact residual local model is Omega_w(d,h,k), the inclusion-exclusion combination of local
singular models Theta_{w,S} over all subsets S of the eight cube vertices. The full eight-form
model Theta_w alone is not the residual cube model.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.


PAGE 261 OF 500
TITLE: Eight-form residual cube
PART: Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint
STATUS: OPEN ANALYTIC ENGINE / REDUCTION
OBJECT: Eight-form residual cube

This page belongs to Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint. Its topic is Eight-
form residual cube. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the
relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as
part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

The direct derivative target sets f=nu-1, B_d(n)=f(n+d)conj(f(n)), c_d=E_n B_d(n), and B_d^circ=B_d-c_d. The derivative endpoint is
(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||B_d^circ||_{U^2}^4=o(1).
Expanding U^2 gives an eight-form residual cube on vertices n+epsilon_1 d+epsilon_2 h+epsilon_3 k, epsilon in {0,1}^3, with zero-frequency subtraction |c_d|^4 and residual local model Omega_w obtained by inclusion-exclusion over all subconfigurations.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

TITLE: Expansion of R_d
PART: Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint
STATUS: OPEN ANALYTIC ENGINE / REDUCTION
OBJECT: Expansion of R_d

This page belongs to Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint. Its topic is
Expansion of R_d. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-

OBJECT: Linear U^2 terms

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or

prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

The centered pair-cube formulation uses R_d(n)=P_d(n)-kappa_w(d), where
kappa_w(d)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-2)(1-#{0,-d mod p}/p).
The endpoint CPC_3^sharp(Hcal) asks for
(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||R_d||_{U^2([N])}^4=o(1),
with sharp or transferred dyadic cutoffs and all side packages.

subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: Unbounded transference burden
PART: Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint
STATUS: OPEN ANALYTIC ENGINE / REDUCTION
OBJECT: Unbounded transference burden

This page belongs to Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint. Its topic is
Unbounded transference burden. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making
the relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read
as part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector

kappa_w(d)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-2)(1-#{0,-d mod p}/p).
The endpoint CPC_3^sharp(Hcal) asks for
(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||R_d||_{U^2([N])}^4=o(1),
with sharp or transferred dyadic cutoffs and all side packages.

This page belongs to Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint. Its topic is Spectral
decomposition of B_d. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
