What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

This page belongs to Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint. Its topic is Centered
derivative B_d^circ. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the
relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as
part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. A hypothetical finite positive selected limit forces q-centered clipped covariance or
q-centered tail covariance on infinitely many dyadic shells, after denominator divergence and
prefix bookkeeping. This is an extraction, not a contradiction.

The direct derivative target sets f=nu-1, B_d(n)=f(n+d)conj(f(n)), c_d=E_n B_d(n), and B_d^circ=B_d-c_d. The derivative endpoint is
(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||B_d^circ||_{U^2}^4=o(1).
Expanding U^2 gives an eight-form residual cube on vertices n+epsilon_1 d+epsilon_2 h+epsilon_3 k, epsilon in {0,1}^3, with zero-frequency subtraction |c_d|^4 and residual local model Omega_w obtained by inclusion-exclusion over all subconfigurations.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

TITLE: Open analytic battlefield
PART: Part IV: Direct Analytic Attack on the Open Endpoint
STATUS: OPEN
OBJECT: RBDH/CPC/AU^3/ResCube

The shortest honest solve target is the zero-frequency-removed derivative cube: average over short d of ||f(.+d)conj(f)-c_d||_{U^2}^4. This expands to an eight-form residual cube. Proving it in the required range would advance the s=2 rational-major branch. It would still not automatically close tail, high-count, harmonic, boundary, W, Liouville, or all-alpha branches unless those are separately routed and controlled.

TITLE: Experiment philosophy
PART: Part V: Computational and Experimental Program
STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: Experiment philosophy

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Experiment
philosophy. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Experiment discipline: computations may estimate selected averages, denominator growth, pair
residuals, rectangle residuals, CPC energies, and residual-cube statistics. They may suggest
conjectures or locate bugs. They do not prove endpoints. Every script must state the selector,
scale, normalization, alpha family, and random seed or deterministic recipe.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:

TITLE: Finite-type alpha grid
PART: Part V: Computational and Experimental Program
STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: Finite-type alpha grid

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Finite-type
alpha grid. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Finite-type screen: if ||u alpha + a/q|| <= rho with q<=Q and |u|<=U, then finite type ||r alpha||>=c_alpha r^{-tau_alpha} forces Q^{tau_alpha+1} U^{tau_alpha} rho >= c_alpha. If this inequality fails, the certificate cannot exist. This screen does not apply to Liouville alpha.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

estimates for free.

conjectures or locate bugs. They do not prove endpoints. Every script must state the selector,
scale, normalization, alpha family, and random seed or deterministic recipe.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

TITLE: Pair and rectangle statistics
PART: Part V: Computational and Experimental Program
STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: Pair and rectangle statistics

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Pair and
rectangle statistics. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the
relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as
part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

For W-tricked nu and P_d(n)=nu(n+d)conj(nu(n)), the endpoint RBDH_pair_short(Hcal) asks, uniformly for dyadic D<=Hcal(N),

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} (1/N) sum_h |
  N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N) P_d(n+h)conj(P_d(n))
  - Sigma_w(d,h) N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N)
|^2 = o(1).

The exact rectangle model is
Sigma_w(d,h)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-4)(1-#{0,-d,-h,-h-d mod p}/p).

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or

STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: Finite-type alpha grid

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Finite-type
alpha grid. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Finite-type screen: if ||u alpha + a/q|| <= rho with q<=Q and |u|<=U, then finite type ||r alpha||>=c_alpha r^{-tau_alpha} forces Q^{tau_alpha+1} U^{tau_alpha} rho >= c_alpha. If this inequality fails, the certificate cannot exist. This screen does not apply to Liouville alpha.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: Liouville proxy alpha grid
PART: Part V: Computational and Experimental Program
STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: Liouville proxy alpha grid

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Liouville
proxy alpha grid. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:

TITLE: Pair and rectangle statistics
PART: Part V: Computational and Experimental Program
STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: Pair and rectangle statistics

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Pair and
rectangle statistics. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the
relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as
part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

For W-tricked nu and P_d(n)=nu(n+d)conj(nu(n)), the endpoint RBDH_pair_short(Hcal) asks, uniformly for dyadic D<=Hcal(N),

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} (1/N) sum_h |
  N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N) P_d(n+h)conj(P_d(n))
  - Sigma_w(d,h) N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N)
|^2 = o(1).

The exact rectangle model is
Sigma_w(d,h)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-4)(1-#{0,-d,-h,-h-d mod p}/p).

 Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where

BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without

TITLE: Experiment philosophy
PART: Part V: Computational and Experimental Program
STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: Experiment philosophy

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Experiment
philosophy. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Experiment discipline: computations may estimate selected averages, denominator growth, pair
residuals, rectangle residuals, CPC energies, and residual-cube statistics. They may suggest
conjectures or locate bugs. They do not prove endpoints. Every script must state the selector,
scale, normalization, alpha family, and random seed or deterministic recipe.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: Prime gap data model
PART: Part V: Computational and Experimental Program
STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: Prime gap data model

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Prime gap data
model. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

TITLE: Finite-type alpha grid
PART: Part V: Computational and Experimental Program
STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: Finite-type alpha grid

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Finite-type
alpha grid. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Finite-type screen: if ||u alpha + a/q|| <= rho with q<=Q and |u|<=U, then finite type ||r alpha||>=c_alpha r^{-tau_alpha} forces Q^{tau_alpha+1} U^{tau_alpha} rho >= c_alpha. If this inequality fails, the certificate cannot exist. This screen does not apply to Liouville alpha.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,

TITLE: Pair and rectangle statistics

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Pair and

For W-tricked nu and P_d(n)=nu(n+d)conj(nu(n)), the endpoint RBDH_pair_short(Hcal) asks, uniformly for dyadic D<=Hcal(N),

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} (1/N) sum_h |
  N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N) P_d(n+h)conj(P_d(n))
  - Sigma_w(d,h) N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N)
|^2 = o(1).

The exact rectangle model is
Sigma_w(d,h)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-4)(1-#{0,-d,-h,-h-d mod p}/p).

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: Finite-type alpha grid
PART: Part V: Computational and Experimental Program
STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: Finite-type alpha grid

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Finite-type
alpha grid. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Finite-type screen: if ||u alpha + a/q|| <= rho with q<=Q and |u|<=U, then finite type ||r alpha||>=c_alpha r^{-tau_alpha} forces Q^{tau_alpha+1} U^{tau_alpha} rho >= c_alpha. If this inequality fails, the certificate cannot exist. This screen does not apply to Liouville alpha.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,

TITLE: Pair and rectangle statistics
PART: Part V: Computational and Experimental Program
STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: Pair and rectangle statistics

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Pair and
rectangle statistics. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the
relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as
part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

For W-tricked nu and P_d(n)=nu(n+d)conj(nu(n)), the endpoint RBDH_pair_short(Hcal) asks, uniformly for dyadic D<=Hcal(N),

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} (1/N) sum_h |
  N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N) P_d(n+h)conj(P_d(n))
  - Sigma_w(d,h) N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N)
|^2 = o(1).

The exact rectangle model is
Sigma_w(d,h)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-4)(1-#{0,-d,-h,-h-d mod p}/p).

 Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without

subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: RBDH residual diagnostics
PART: Part V: Computational and Experimental Program
STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: RBDH residual diagnostics

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is RBDH residual
diagnostics. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

For W-tricked nu and P_d(n)=nu(n+d)conj(nu(n)), the endpoint RBDH_pair_short(Hcal) asks, uniformly for dyadic D<=Hcal(N),

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} (1/N) sum_h |
  N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N) P_d(n+h)conj(P_d(n))
  - Sigma_w(d,h) N^{-1} sum_n Phi(n/N,h/N)
|^2 = o(1).

The exact rectangle model is
Sigma_w(d,h)=prod_{p>w}(1-1/p)^(-4)(1-#{0,-d,-h,-h-d mod p}/p).

 Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:

STATUS: HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC
OBJECT: CPC diagnostics

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is CPC
diagnostics. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-

The endpoint CPC_3^sharp(Hcal) asks for
(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||R_d||_{U^2([N])}^4=o(1),
with sharp or transferred dyadic cutoffs and all side packages.

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is ResCube

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-

(1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} ||B_d^circ||_{U^2}^4=o(1).
Expanding U^2 gives an eight-form residual cube on vertices n+epsilon_1 d+epsilon_2 h+epsilon_3 k, epsilon in {0,1}^3, with zero-frequency subtraction |c_d|^4 and residual local model Omega_w obtained by inclusion-exclusion over all subconfigurations.

OBJECT: Visualization plan

This page belongs to Part V: Computational and Experimental Program. Its topic is Visualization

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

scale, normalization, alpha family, and random seed or deterministic recipe.

REPOSITORY PLAN FOR https://github.com/flaritycat/PrimeResonanceGap

Root files:
- README.md: global status dashboard, immediate next work, and no-overclaim warning.

- docs/status/global_status.md
- docs/selectors.md
- docs/gaps.md
- docs/endpoints/s2_endpoint_class.md
- docs/endpoints/rescube3_sharp.md

- docs/papers/paper_II_conditional_finite_type.md
- docs/papers/paper_III_s2_endpoint_compression.md
- docs/modules/module_156_*.md through current module files.

- experiments/README.md
- experiments/prime_gap_data.md
- experiments/rbdh_diagnostics.md
- experiments/cpc_diagnostics.md
- experiments/rescube_diagnostics.md

The repository must make overclaiming difficult. A surprisingly ambitious goal, given the species.

The repository layout is documentation infrastructure. It does not prove any endpoint.

range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: experiments

STATUS: STRUCTURAL / DOCUMENTATION
OBJECT: experiments

This page belongs to Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan. Its topic is
experiments. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Experiment discipline: computations may estimate selected averages, denominator growth, pair
residuals, rectangle residuals, CPC energies, and residual-cube statistics. They may suggest
conjectures or locate bugs. They do not prove endpoints. Every script must state the selector,
scale, normalization, alpha family, and random seed or deterministic recipe.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-

completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,

BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Selector warning: the actual selector is sharp and moving; frozen and smoothed versions require transfer.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: experiments
PART: Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan
STATUS: STRUCTURAL / DOCUMENTATION
OBJECT: experiments

This page belongs to Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan. Its topic is
experiments. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Experiment discipline: computations may estimate selected averages, denominator growth, pair
residuals, rectangle residuals, CPC energies, and residual-cube statistics. They may suggest
conjectures or locate bugs. They do not prove endpoints. Every script must state the selector,
scale, normalization, alpha family, and random seed or deterministic recipe.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or

TITLE: Repository structure
PART: Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan
STATUS: STRUCTURAL / DOCUMENTATION
OBJECT: Repository structure

This page belongs to Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan. Its topic is
Repository structure. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the
relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as
part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Repository documentation target: https://github.com/flaritycat/PrimeResonanceGap. Recommended
files: README.md, STATUS.md, docs/status/global_status.md, docs/selectors.md, docs/gaps.md,
docs/endpoints/s2_endpoint_class.md, docs/endpoints/rescube3_sharp.md, docs/modules/,
docs/papers/, experiments/, scripts/, tests/, and .github/.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: README status dashboard
PART: Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan
STATUS: STRUCTURAL / DOCUMENTATION
OBJECT: README status dashboard

This page belongs to Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan. Its topic is README
status dashboard. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: docs/selectors
PART: Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan
STATUS: STRUCTURAL / DOCUMENTATION
OBJECT: docs/selectors

This page belongs to Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan. Its topic is
docs/selectors. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Selector warning: the actual selector is sharp and moving; frozen and smoothed versions require transfer.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: experiments
PART: Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan
STATUS: STRUCTURAL / DOCUMENTATION
OBJECT: experiments

This page belongs to Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan. Its topic is
experiments. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Experiment discipline: computations may estimate selected averages, denominator growth, pair
residuals, rectangle residuals, CPC energies, and residual-cube statistics. They may suggest
conjectures or locate bugs. They do not prove endpoints. Every script must state the selector,
scale, normalization, alpha family, and random seed or deterministic recipe.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

STATUS: STRUCTURAL / DOCUMENTATION
OBJECT: issue templates

This page belongs to Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan. Its topic is issue
templates. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

PAGE 424 OF 500
TITLE: experiments
PART: Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan
STATUS: STRUCTURAL / DOCUMENTATION
OBJECT: experiments

This page belongs to Part VI: GitHub Documentation and Formalization Plan. Its topic is
experiments. The purpose is to preserve the project architecture while making the relevant
definitions, assumptions, reductions, and open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a
status-preserving paper, not as a proof of the original open problem.

Claim. Experiments can measure and falsify naive heuristics, but they cannot replace variance-
strength analytic estimates. Every diagnostic must be labeled HEURISTIC / DIAGNOSTIC.

Experiment discipline: computations may estimate selected averages, denominator growth, pair
residuals, rectangle residuals, CPC energies, and residual-cube statistics. They may suggest
conjectures or locate bugs. They do not prove endpoints. Every script must state the selector,
scale, normalization, alpha family, and random seed or deterministic recipe.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-

completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

 BEGIN CODEX CONTINUATION PROMPT Repository: https://github.com/flaritycat/PrimeResonanceGap
Project: Prime Gap Resonance Project

You are continuing a mathematical research ledger, not polishing a solved theorem. Preserve status labels exactly: PROVEN, CONDITIONAL, STRUCTURAL / EXTRACTION, HEURISTIC, OPEN, FALSE / BLOCKED. The original positive existence problem is OPEN. The all-alpha no-positive-limit theorem is OPEN. The metric theorem A_alpha(x)->1 for Lebesgue-a.e. irrational alpha is PROVEN according to the ledger. The finite-type theorem is CONDITIONAL. The s=2 analytic endpoint RBDH_pair_short / CPC_3^sharp / AU^3 is OPEN.

Do not upgrade any smoothed, frozen, finite-stage, Bernoulli, or model statement to the actual sharp moving selector chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p} unless all transfer packages are explicitly present: denominator divergence, scalar feedback, prefix safety, tail control, boundary transfer, transition control, irrational survivor extraction, W-trick limit order, prime-power transfer, and all recursive engines.

Current best s=2 endpoint class:
RBDH_pair_short(Hcal) <=> CPC_3^sharp(Hcal) <=> SPAC_2^sharp <=> SU2Pair_2^sharp <=> DyadicDerivativeU^2 <=> AU^3,
modulo exact local pair model kappa_w(d), exact rectangle model Sigma_w(d,h), two-rectangle model Theta_w(d,h,k), local covariance L^2 calibration, pair-margin absorption, linear U^2 for nu-1, boundary transfer, collision control, W-trick limit order, prime-power transfer, and range coverage D<=Hcal(N).

Immediate tasks:
1. Create or update README.md with the global status dashboard and claim discipline.
2. Create docs/status/global_status.md, docs/selectors.md, docs/gaps.md, docs/endpoints/s2_endpoint_class.md, docs/endpoints/rescube3_sharp.md, docs/papers/paper_I_structural_obstruction.md, docs/papers/paper_II_conditional_finite_type.md, docs/papers/paper_III_s2_endpoint_compression.md.
3. Add a module ledger under docs/modules/ with continuation modules after the last committed module. Each module must include: precise claim, status, definitions, assumptions, proof/reduction, edge cases, project-map location, remaining open work.
4. Add experiments/README.md and scripts only for empirical diagnostics. Never present experiments as proof.
5. If attacking the endpoint, focus on ResCube_3^sharp: prove or reduce the zero-frequency-removed eight-form residual cube estimate for f=nu-1, B_d(n)=f(n+d)conj(f(n)), and B_d^circ=B_d-c_d.
6. Write every theorem with its selector, gap object, local model, normalization, range, and transfer assumptions.
7. Do not claim the endpoint is proved unless a complete variance-strength proof is supplied in the required range.

Required output style: - Commit small, auditable files. - Include status labels in headings. -
Prefer theorem dependency tables to prose optimism. - Add red-flag sections for forbidden
upgrades. - Keep equations in plain text or LaTeX-compatible Markdown.

Start by reading docs/status/global_status.md if it exists. If not, create it from the status
dashboard above. Then create a branch named codex/endpoint-rescube-ledger and add the next
continuation module as docs/modules/module_179_rescube_spectral_decomposition.md. END CODEX
CONTINUATION PROMPT

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

PAGE 475 OF 500
TITLE: Proposed Module 179
PART: Part VII: Codex Continuation Prompt and Agent Protocol
STATUS: STRUCTURAL / EXTRACTION; ANALYTIC TARGET OPEN
OBJECT: Next continuation module

PROPOSED NEXT MODULE: Module 179, Spectral decomposition of the centered derivative cube

Precise claim. The endpoint ResCube_3^sharp(Hcal) may be reformulated as a nonzero-frequency fourth moment for the derivative Fourier coefficients of B_d(n)=f(n+d)conj(f(n)).

Status. STRUCTURAL / EXTRACTION for the reformulation; OPEN for the analytic estimate.

Definitions. f=nu-1, B_d=f(.+d)conj(f), c_d=E B_d, B_d^circ=B_d-c_d, and E_d(xi)=hat{B_d}(xi) for xi nonzero.

Target. Prove (1/D) sum_{D<|d|<=2D} sum_{xi != 0} |E_d(xi)|^4=o(1) in all required ranges, with W-trick, boundary, collision, prime-power, and local-model side packages.

Proof map. Use Parseval and U^2 identities to identify this estimate with the centered derivative cube. Then split xi into major and minor arcs. Major arcs require exact residual local model subtraction. Minor arcs require pair-product spectral-density cancellation.

What remains open. Both major and minor arc analytic estimates remain open. This module should be the first Codex continuation file after the present paper.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not call Module 179 a proof of the endpoint unless the nonzero-frequency fourth moment is actually proved.

completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

TITLE: Glossary of selectors

STATUS: REFERENCE / STATUS DISCIPLINE
OBJECT: Glossary of selectors

This page belongs to Appendices. Its topic is Glossary of selectors. The purpose is to preserve
the project architecture while making the relevant definitions, assumptions, reductions, and
open engines explicit. It should be read as part of a status-preserving paper, not as a proof of
the original open problem.

Claim. The appendices collect checklists, glossary entries, endpoint tables, source-engine
tables, and forbidden upgrades. They are reference infrastructure, not new theorems.

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Selector warning: the actual selector is sharp and moving; frozen and smoothed versions require transfer.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-

subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-

Prime gaps: p_n is the n-th prime, Delta_n=p_{n+1}-p_n, g_n=Delta_n/log p_n. Prime-variable notation: p^+ is the next prime after p, and g(p)=(p^+-p)/log p. Actual selector: chi_alpha(p)=1_{||alpha p||<1/log p}. Selected denominator D_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p). Selected numerator N_alpha(x)=sum_{p<=x} chi_alpha(p) g(p). Selected average A_alpha(x)=N_alpha(x)/D_alpha(x), when D_alpha(x)>0.

Proof / reduction map. Start from the exact object, subtract the exact local model where
required, keep the dyadic range explicit, and route any surviving term to a named branch or
endpoint. If the page asserts an equivalence, list side packages. If the page asserts closure,
list the analytic engine. If no engine is proved, preserve OPEN or CONDITIONAL status.

Edge cases. Watch for denominator failure, boundary strips, collision hyperplanes, W-trick limit
order, prime powers, model mismatch, high-count remainders, Liouville exceptions, and large-
range escape. These are not footnotes; they are where false proofs like to rent space.

What remains open. The project still needs endpoint proofs in the required ranges, source
completeness, explicit source envelopes, actual-selector transfer, tail and high-count closure,
Liouville short-comb analysis, and large-range control where relevant.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.

Forbidden shortcuts: Pair-BDH alone implies CPC_3^sharp; mean rectangle-HL implies rectangle-
BDH; unweighted first-moment HL implies RBDH; raw shifted-pair Fourier smallness without
subtracting kappa_w(d); replacing Sigma_w(d,h) by kappa_w(d)^2 pointwise; generic finite-
complexity HL automatically closing the endpoint; smoothed estimates implying sharp selector
estimates for free.

DISCIPLINE / CAUTION:
Do not promote this page beyond its status label. Do not state that the original positive problem, the all-alpha theorem, the unconditional finite-type theorem, or the s=2 analytic endpoint has been solved unless a complete proof is supplied.
